checks.name: No hardcoded secrets
checks.plain_english: Are passwords/API keys written directly in code instead of environment variables?
checks.fix: Move to environment variables; add to .env.example with placeholder.

checks.name: Environment variable hygiene
checks.plain_english: Are credentials properly hidden from users' browsers?
checks.fix: Add missing .env.example; remove any NEXT_PUBLIC_ secret references.

checks.name: Dependency vulnerabilities
checks.plain_english: Do any libraries we use have known security holes?
checks.fix: Upgrade vulnerable dependencies; replace unmaintained packages.

checks.name: CORS configuration
checks.plain_english: Does the server only accept requests from our own websites?
checks.fix: Set allow_origins to a specific list, never '*'.

checks.name: Authentication coverage
checks.evidence: V1.5: auth deferred per product decision (public dev deployment); check waived
checks.plain_english: Can anyone access data without logging in?
checks.fix: Implement auth layer when product decision changes (post-V10).

checks.name: Supabase service role key
checks.evidence: ATLAS uses PostgreSQL RDS directly, not Supabase
checks.plain_english: Is the master database key safely locked away?

checks.name: HTTPS enforcement
checks.evidence: no nginx config yet (set up in C8)
checks.plain_english: Is all traffic encrypted?
checks.fix: Add nginx HTTP→HTTPS redirect + Let's Encrypt cert.

checks.name: Rate limiting
checks.plain_english: If someone tries to overwhelm us with requests, do we have protection?
checks.fix: Add slowapi middleware to public routes.

checks.name: Input validation
checks.plain_english: Do we validate data coming in, or blindly trust it?
checks.fix: Use Pydantic models for all request bodies; never raw dict.

checks.name: No API key leaks in runner logs / spec files
checks.evidence: no runner log API key leaks
checks.plain_english: Did any Anthropic API key leak into forge-runner log files or specs?
checks.fix: Verify secrets.scrub() is called on every log write; rotate any leaked key immediately.

checks.name: Zero lint errors
checks.plain_english: Does the code follow formatting/style rules?

checks.name: Zero type errors
checks.plain_english: Is the code type-safe?
checks.fix: Add type hints; fix mypy errors.

checks.name: File modularity
checks.plain_english: Are features in small modules or crammed into huge files?
checks.fix: Split files > 500 lines into smaller modules.

checks.name: Function size and complexity
checks.plain_english: Are functions small and focused, or monster functions?
checks.fix: Break up large/complex functions into smaller helpers.

checks.name: Naming quality
checks.plain_english: Can you understand what each thing does from its name?
checks.fix: Rename generic variables to descriptive names.

checks.name: No dead code
checks.plain_english: Leftover unused code?

checks.name: Error handling
checks.plain_english: Does the system handle errors gracefully?
checks.fix: Replace print() with structlog; narrow except clauses; log errors.

checks.name: Consistent formatting
checks.plain_english: Is code formatted consistently?

checks.name: API response consistency
checks.plain_english: Do endpoints return consistent shapes?
checks.fix: Use response_model= on all routes; return Pydantic models.

checks.name: No TODO/FIXME markers
checks.plain_english: Are there 'fix this later' notes that never got fixed?
checks.fix: Turn each TODO into a tracked issue then remove the marker.

checks.name: Layered structure (routes/core/models/db)
checks.plain_english: Are HTTP, business logic, and data access separated?
checks.fix: Create routes/ core/ models/ db/ folders.

checks.name: Routes don't query JIP de_* tables directly
checks.plain_english: JIP tables must be accessed via JIP /internal/ client, not direct SQL.

checks.name: JIP client abstraction present
checks.plain_english: Is JIP access centralized through a client module?

checks.name: Decimal not float
checks.plain_english: Financial values must be Decimal, never float.
checks.fix: Replace float with Decimal in all financial code.

checks.name: Structured logging
checks.plain_english: Is logging structured and queryable?
checks.fix: Use structlog with context keys, never print().

checks.name: Migrations via Alembic
checks.plain_english: Are schema changes versioned?
checks.fix: Initialize Alembic and manage all DDL through migrations.

checks.name: README present
checks.plain_english: Does the README explain what this is and how to run it?

checks.name: CLAUDE.md present and live
checks.plain_english: Is there project-specific Claude guidance?
checks.fix: Expand CLAUDE.md with stack, rules, decisions.

checks.name: Architecture decisions recorded
checks.plain_english: Are key decisions documented?
checks.fix: Write ADRs under docs/adr/ or expand CLAUDE.md.

checks.name: Standards doc matches code
checks.plain_english: Does the rubric still describe the engine that runs?

checks.name: OpenAPI inventory
checks.plain_english: Is the API spec self-documenting?
checks.fix: Export FastAPI OpenAPI at build time.

checks.name: Endpoint response time
checks.plain_english: Are API responses fast enough for users?
checks.fix: Profile slow endpoints; add DB indexes or cache.

checks.name: Error rate
checks.plain_english: Are endpoints returning success?

checks.name: Response format compliance
checks.plain_english: Do responses follow a consistent JSON contract?
checks.fix: Ensure all endpoints return application/json with valid bodies.

checks.name: DB query performance
checks.plain_english: Are DB-backed endpoints fast?
checks.fix: Add indexes on filter columns; review slow query log.

checks.name: Build succeeds
checks.plain_english: Does the frontend build cleanly?

checks.name: Component modularity
checks.plain_english: Are components small and focused?

checks.name: Frontend criteria gate
checks.plain_english: Does the frontend meet V1 acceptance criteria?
checks.fix: Run python scripts/check-frontend-criteria.py to see failures.

checks.name: Accessibility
checks.plain_english: Static HTML a11y checks (alt attrs, lang, viewport, duplicate IDs)
checks.fix: Run python scripts/check-fe-a11y.py to see issues.

checks.name: Alembic head matches models
checks.evidence: drift detected: FAILED: New upgrade operations detected: [('remove_index', Index('idx_run_tags', Column('key', TEXT(), table=<run_tags>)
checks.plain_english: Are DB migrations in sync with models?

checks.name: Docker build file present
checks.plain_english: Is the service containerized?

checks.name: Alembic configured
checks.plain_english: Are schema changes versioned?

checks.name: pip-audit clean
checks.plain_english: Any known vulnerabilities in Python deps?

checks.name: Pipeline idempotent
checks.evidence: backend/pipelines/daily.py not found — informational until V1.6

checks.name: Intelligence writes on run
checks.evidence: pipeline not present — informational until V1.6

checks.name: Decisions generated on run
checks.evidence: pipeline not present — informational until V1.6

checks.name: CI/CD workflows
checks.plain_english: Is there automated CI/CD?

checks.name: /stocks/universe returns valid data matching contract
checks.plain_english: GET /api/v1/stocks/universe returns 200 under the 2s budget.

checks.name: /stocks/sectors returns 31 sectors with 22 metrics each
checks.plain_english: Each sector row must carry at least 22 metric columns beyond the sector label.

checks.name: /stocks/{symbol} returns deep-dive for any stock
checks.plain_english: GET /api/v1/stocks/RELIANCE returns 200 under the 500ms budget.

checks.name: /query handles basic equity queries (filters + sort)
checks.plain_english: POST /api/v1/query with a minimal filter returns 200.

checks.name: Frontend: FM navigates Market → Sector → Stock flow
checks.plain_english: Key frontend pages/components for the FM flow exist in the build.

checks.name: Deep-dive panel shows all conviction pillar data
checks.plain_english: DeepDivePanel component exists and references the conviction pillars.

checks.name: ≥5 decisions generated per pipeline run
checks.plain_english: atlas_decisions has at least 5 rows from the last 24h pipeline run.

checks.name: FM can accept / ignore / override decisions
checks.plain_english: Decisions endpoint exposed and decisions route module present.

checks.name: Sector rollup stock_count sums to ~2,700
checks.plain_english: Sum of stock_count across /sectors must land in [2500, 2900].

checks.name: RS momentum present and numeric on deep-dive
checks.plain_english: GET /stocks/RELIANCE carries an rs_momentum field convertible to float.

checks.name: pct_above_200dma exposed on /sectors and in [0,100]
checks.plain_english: Every sector row must carry pct_above_200dma in [0,100].

checks.name: ≥10 intelligence findings stored after first pipeline run
checks.plain_english: atlas_intelligence has at least 10 rows from the last 24h pipeline run.

checks.name: Integration test suite present
checks.plain_english: tests/integration/test_v1_endpoints.py exists and is non-trivial.

checks.name: No float in financial calculations
checks.plain_english: AST scan of backend/ finds zero `: float` annotations in money-handling code.

checks.name: Response times: universe < 2s, deep-dive < 500ms
checks.plain_english: Re-run both critical endpoints against their latency budgets in one check.

checks.name: POST /query supports group_by + aggregations
checks.plain_english: §17.5 — query must accept group_by + aggregations with functions avg, sum, count, pct_true, pct_above, pct_below, median, stddev. Response rows must carry the aliased aggregate columns.

checks.name: POST /query supports mode=timeseries with time_range
checks.plain_english: §17.6 — time-series mode must accept time_range.from/to + granularity and return date-indexed rows for the requested fields.

checks.name: POST /query/template dispatches named templates
checks.plain_english: §17.7 — a separate template endpoint must accept {template, params} and execute predefined UQL queries (top_rs_gainers, sector_rotation, breadth_dashboard, etc). Adding a template must require no route code.

checks.name: GET /stocks/{symbol}?include=rs,conviction composes response
checks.plain_english: §18 — the include= parameter must attach requested modules and the response must declare `_meta.includes_loaded` with the resolved list.

checks.name: Fixed endpoints route through the UQL service
checks.plain_english: §17.10 — GET /stocks/universe, /stocks/sectors, /stocks/movers etc. must be thin wrappers that construct a UQL query and call the shared execution engine. Static check: a `backend/services/uql` module must exist and be imported by `backend/routes/stocks.py`.

checks.name: GET /intelligence/findings returns standard envelope with _meta
checks.plain_english: §20.4 — list endpoints must return {data: [...], _meta: {...}} so the standard checker can introspect pagination and provenance metadata.

checks.name: GET /intelligence/findings returns data array
checks.plain_english: §20.4 — the data key must be present and be an array (may be empty). check-api-standard.py _first_record() looks for data/records/rows/items.

checks.name: GET /intelligence/search module exposes standard envelope shape
checks.plain_english: §20.4 — static check that intelligence route imports IntelligenceSearchResponse and FindingSummaryEnvelope (envelope models) from backend.models.schemas. Live probe skipped because the search endpoint requires the embedding service (Ollama / OpenAI) to be available.

checks.name: Errors carry code + message + suggestion
checks.plain_english: §20.5 — malformed queries must return a structured error with {code, message, suggestion} so FMs and agents get self-explaining failures, not a bare 422.

checks.name: /mf/universe returns valid MF data
checks.plain_english: GET /api/v1/mf/universe returns 200 under the 2s budget with fund data.

checks.name: /mf/categories returns category hierarchy
checks.plain_english: GET /api/v1/mf/categories returns 200 with category groups.

checks.name: /mf/{mstar_id} deep-dive returns fund data (SC-003: idempotent)
checks.plain_english: GET /api/v1/mf/{mstar_id} with a real mstar_id returns 200 with fund data. Two consecutive calls return identical structure — confirming idempotency. Maps to SC-003 (idempotent re-run produces consistent output).

checks.name: /mf/categories response includes data_as_of staleness (SC-009: structured data)
checks.plain_english: Categories response must include staleness metadata (data_as_of or staleness field). Maps to SC-009 (structured provenance in pipeline outputs).

checks.name: MF Pro shell page exists with category/universe/flows/deep-dive (SC-004: FM UI)
checks.plain_english: Key frontend file frontend/src/app/page.tsx exists and is non-trivial (>=500 bytes). Maps to SC-004 (FM can use the UI — MF page components exist in frontend).

checks.name: MF decisions generated in atlas_decisions (SC-001 + SC-002)
checks.plain_english: atlas_decisions has at least 1 row from any MF-related source type (mf_quadrant_transition, mf_flow_reversal, or any decision in last 7 days). Maps to SC-001 (pipeline findings >=10) and SC-002 (pipeline decisions >=5).

checks.name: No float in MF financial calculations (SC-008)
checks.evidence: 0 float annotations in 2 MF backend files
checks.plain_english: AST scan of backend/routes/mf.py and backend/services/mf_compute.py finds zero ': float' annotations in money-handling code. Maps to SC-008 (no float in financial calculations).

checks.name: All V1 criteria pass (SC-006)
checks.evidence: V1 validate exited 0: All criteria PASSED -- V1 complete
checks.plain_english: Running validate-v1-completion.py exits 0, confirming all 15 V1 criteria remain green. Maps to SC-006 (all V1 criteria pass).

checks.name: MF response times within budget: universe < 2s, deep-dive < 500ms (SC-005)
checks.plain_english: Both /mf/universe (budget 2000ms) and /mf/{mstar_id} (budget 500ms) respond within their latency budgets. Maps to SC-005 (API latency p95 < budget).

checks.name: atlas_simulations table exists with required columns
checks.plain_english: The atlas_simulations table must exist with all spec §8 columns plus updated_at and is_deleted (project convention).

checks.name: Partial index on atlas_simulations for auto-loop queries exists
checks.plain_english: The partial index ix_atlas_simulations_auto_loop on (user_id, is_auto_loop) WHERE is_auto_loop = TRUE AND is_deleted = FALSE must exist for fast scheduling.

checks.name: POST /api/v1/simulate/run endpoint exists and returns non-5xx
checks.plain_english: The simulation run endpoint must be registered. The probe POSTs a minimal shell payload — anything except 404/5xx counts as "endpoint exists". Acceptable: 200/201 (executed), 400/422 (validation rejected the shell payload, route reachable), 501 (stub). 404/5xx are failures. Maps to spec §11 (API Layer — every endpoint).

checks.name: No float in simulation financial models (Decimal-only)
checks.plain_english: AST scan of backend/models/simulation.py and backend/services/simulation/ finds zero ': float' annotations. All financial values must be Decimal.

checks.name: No print() in backend/services/simulation/ (structlog only)
checks.evidence: 0 print() calls across 12 simulation service files
checks.plain_english: AST scan of backend/services/simulation/ finds zero print() calls. All logging must go through structlog per project conventions.

checks.name: atlas_portfolios table exists with required columns
atlas_portfolio_holdings table exists with required columns
POST /api/v1/portfolio/create endpoint exists
checks.plain_english: The portfolio create endpoint must be registered. The probe POSTs a minimal shell payload — 200/201 (created), 400/422 (validation rejected, route reachable), or 501 (stub) all count as success. 404/5xx fail.

checks.name: No print() in portfolio code (structlog only)
atlas_intelligence table exists with required columns
checks.plain_english: The atlas_intelligence table must exist with all core columns: id, agent_id, agent_type, entity, entity_type, finding_type, title, content, confidence, evidence, embedding, tags, data_as_of, expires_at, is_validated, validation_result, created_at, updated_at. min: 18 (excludes soft-delete columns).

checks.name: HNSW vector index exists on atlas_intelligence
checks.plain_english: The HNSW index idx_intelligence_embedding must exist on the atlas_intelligence table for fast vector similarity search.

checks.name: atlas_agent_scores table exists with required columns
checks.plain_english: The atlas_agent_scores table must exist with core tracking columns: id, agent_id, accuracy_score, prediction_date, entity, actual_outcome, evaluation_date, created_at, updated_at. This table supports the Darwinian scorer pipeline.

checks.name: atlas_cost_ledger table exists with required columns
checks.plain_english: The atlas_cost_ledger table must exist with columns for tracking LLM API costs: id, agent_id, model, cost_usd, total_tokens, created_at, updated_at.

checks.name: GET /api/v1/intelligence/findings endpoint exists
checks.plain_english: The intelligence findings list endpoint must be registered. Acceptable: 200/400/422/501. Failures: 404/5xx.

checks.name: GET /api/v1/intelligence/search endpoint exists
checks.plain_english: The intelligence vector search endpoint must be registered. Acceptable: 200/400/422/501. Failures: 404/5xx.

checks.name: GET /api/v1/global/briefing endpoint exists
checks.plain_english: The global briefing endpoint must be registered and return non-404/5xx. Acceptable: 200/400/422/501.

checks.name: GET /api/v1/global/regime endpoint exists
checks.plain_english: The global regime endpoint must be registered and return non-404/5xx. Acceptable: 200/400/422/501.

checks.name: GET /api/v1/global/rs-heatmap endpoint exists
checks.plain_english: The RS heatmap endpoint must be registered and return non-404/5xx. Acceptable: 200/400/422/501.

checks.name: No float in V5 intelligence/agent code (Decimal-only)
checks.plain_english: AST scan of backend/services/intelligence.py, backend/services/embedding.py, backend/services/cost_ledger.py, and backend/agents/ finds zero ': float' annotations. All financial values must be Decimal.

checks.name: No print() in V5 backend code (structlog only)
checks.plain_english: AST scan of the same V5 directories finds zero print() calls. All logging must go through structlog per project conventions.

checks.name: Cost ledger budget gate exists
checks.plain_english: backend/services/cost_ledger.py must contain DAILY_BUDGET_USD constant and BudgetExhaustedError exception class, enforcing the $2/day hard gate.

checks.name: atlas_tv_cache table has required columns
checks.plain_english: The atlas_tv_cache table must exist with all core columns: symbol, data_type, interval, data, fetched_at. These columns define the composite PK and JSONB payload.

checks.name: atlas_watchlists table has required columns
checks.plain_english: The atlas_watchlists table must exist with all core columns: id, name, symbols, tv_synced, is_deleted, created_at, updated_at. These columns support the watchlist CRUD and TV sync features.

checks.name: atlas_alerts table has required columns
checks.plain_english: The atlas_alerts table must exist with all core columns: id, symbol, source, message, is_read, created_at, updated_at. These columns support the alerts API and frontend alerts page.

checks.name: atlas_alerts table has at least 10 columns
checks.plain_english: The atlas_alerts table must exist with at least 10 columns supporting the alerts API (including metadata, rs_at_alert, quadrant_at_alert, alert_type, instrument_id, is_deleted).

checks.name: GET /api/tv/ta/{symbol} endpoint reachable
checks.plain_english: The TradingView technical analysis endpoint for a single symbol must be registered. Acceptable statuses: 200/400/422/503. 503 is acceptable when the TV bridge is offline.

checks.name: GET /api/tv/screener/{symbol} endpoint reachable
checks.plain_english: The TradingView screener data endpoint for a single symbol must be registered. Acceptable statuses: 200/400/422/503.

checks.name: GET /api/tv/fundamentals/{symbol} endpoint reachable
checks.plain_english: The TradingView fundamentals endpoint for a single symbol must be registered. Acceptable statuses: 200/400/422/503.

checks.name: GET /api/tv/ta/bulk bulk endpoint reachable (not 503)
checks.plain_english: The TV bulk TA lookup endpoint must be registered and not return 503. Acceptable statuses: 200/400/422. 503 is NOT acceptable here because the bulk endpoint uses cached data and must not depend on live TV.

checks.name: POST /api/webhooks/tradingview returns 403 without secret
checks.plain_english: The TradingView webhook endpoint must reject requests without the X-Tradingview-Secret header, returning 403 Forbidden. This enforces the webhook security guard.

checks.name: POST /api/v1/watchlists/{id}/sync-tv returns 404 (removed)
checks.plain_english: The sync-tv sub-route was removed in V6T-2. POSTing to it with a dummy UUID must return 404, not 200/422. This validates the removal is in effect and no stale route is registered.

checks.name: GET /api/v1/watchlists/ returns 200
checks.plain_english: The watchlists list endpoint must be registered and return 200. This is a core CRUD endpoint required by the pro watchlists page.

checks.name: GET /api/alerts returns 200
checks.plain_english: The alerts list endpoint must be registered and return 200. This is a core endpoint required by the pro alerts page.

checks.name: No httpx import in bridge.py (asyncio.to_thread bridge)
checks.evidence: bridge.py has no httpx imports (asyncio.to_thread bridge confirmed)
checks.plain_english: backend/services/tv/bridge.py must not import httpx. The V6T-2 migration replaced the httpx sidecar with direct tradingview_screener library calls via asyncio.to_thread. Any httpx import is a regression.

checks.name: tradingview-screener pinned in requirements.txt
checks.plain_english: backend/requirements.txt must contain tradingview-screener with an exact version pin (e.g. tradingview-screener==3.1.0). Unpinned dependencies risk breaking the TV integration on deploy.

checks.name: No float annotations in V6 backend files (Decimal-only)
checks.plain_english: AST scan of V6 backend files (bridge.py, cache_service.py, alert.py, watchlist.py, routes/tv.py, routes/alerts.py, routes/watchlists.py, routes/webhooks.py) finds zero ': float' type annotations. All financial values must use Decimal.

checks.name: No print() calls in V6 backend files (structlog only)
checks.plain_english: AST scan of the same V6 backend files finds zero print() calls. All logging must go through structlog per project conventions.

checks.name: Frontend /pro/watchlists/page.tsx exists
checks.plain_english: The pro watchlists page component must exist at frontend/src/app/pro/watchlists/page.tsx. This page is required for the watchlist management UI.

checks.name: Frontend /pro/alerts/page.tsx exists
checks.plain_english: The pro alerts page component must exist at frontend/src/app/pro/alerts/page.tsx. This page is required for the alerts management UI.

checks.name: Frontend src/lib/tv.ts exists
checks.plain_english: The TV API client library must exist at frontend/src/lib/tv.ts. This module provides the typed fetch helpers for TV data.

checks.name: Frontend TVConvictionPanel.tsx exists
checks.plain_english: The TV Conviction Panel component must exist somewhere under frontend/src/components/. This component renders the TV TA conviction pillar in deep-dive.
